content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 7, 1995

James Phipps
Commission on Improving
Life Through Service
1121 L Street, Ste. 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-242

Dear Mr. Phipps:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Commission on
Improving Life Through Service ("CILTS") regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Does the Act require CILTS to designate its Honorary Chair in
its proposed conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION

The Honorary Chair of CILTS does not make governmental
decisions; therefore, it is not necessary for CILTS to include
this position in its conflict of interest code.

FACTS

Mrs. Gayle Wilson is the Honorary Chair of CILTS. The
Honorary Chair does not participate in commission meetings and
does not have voting rights. In her capacity as Honorary Chair,
Mrs. Wilson has made public service announcements on behalf of

_______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Phipps, James
File No. A-95-242
Page 2

CILTS and has represented CILTS in ceremonial functions at public
events including conferences. Mrs. Wilson endeavors to increase
public awareness of CILTS and attempts to foster its goal of
promoting the service ethic in California.

ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 87300 requires every agency to adopt
and promulgate a conflict of interest code. A conflict of
interest code is a document which specifically enumerates each of
the positions within the agency which involve the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest. (Section
87302.) Persons holding positions enumerated in the code must
file periodic statements disclosing certain personal financial
interests.

Commission Regulation 18700 defines what it means to make or
participate in the making of governmental decisions. Section
18700(b) provides that a public official makes a governmental
decision when he or she, acting within the authority of his or her
office:

(1) Votes on a matter;

(2) Appoints a person;

(3) Obligates or commits his or her agency to any course of
action;

(4) Enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his
or her agency;

Based on the information you have provided, the Honorary
Chair of CILTS does not make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions. Therefore, the position of Honorary Chair
need not be included in the conflict of interest code of CILTS.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
[signature]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question:
Does the Act require CILTS to designate its Honorary Chair in
its proposed conflict of interest code?
sections.conclusion:
The Honorary Chair of CILTS does not make governmental
decisions; therefore, it is not necessary for CILTS to include
this position in its conflict of interest code.
-
-

QUESTION: Does the Act require CILTS to designate its Honorary Chair in
its proposed conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION: The Honorary Chair of CILTS does not make governmental
decisions; therefore, it is not necessary for CILTS to include
this position in its conflict of interest code.
